verifier_values.criteria: States Liverbird was in Breach of the ENA for failing to wire the money by the 11/12 deadline
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: The city had the right to demand the extra $25,000 under Section 3.2 of the ENA. So Liverbird was required to wire the money by the 11/12 deadline. However, the email thread shows Liverbird wired the money on 11/19 so they were in Breach of the ENA under Section 12.1(a) for failing to perform an obligation under the agreement.

verifier_values.criteria: States that Liverbird properly cured the Breach by making the payment by 11/19
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Per Section 12.2(a), for monetary breaches, the breaching party has 5 business days to cure their breach. 5 business days after 11/12 is 11/19. By making the payment by 11/19, Liverbird cured their Breach under Section 12.2(a).

verifier_values.criteria: States that since they properly cured the Breach, no Default occurred
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Liverbird cured their Breach, thus the Breach did not turn into a Default under Section 12.2.

verifier_values.criteria: States that Myric's termination of the final exclusive negotiating agreement is not valid under Section 12.4(a)
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: Myric cannot terminate the agreement under Section 12.4(a) without a Default occurring. A Default did not occur since Liverbird cured their Breach prior to it becoming a Default so they cannot terminate the agreement under Section 12.4(a).
